title: Kubernetes API Concepts — API verbs
text:
Almost all object resource types support the standard HTTP verbs - GET, POST, PUT, PATCH,
and DELETE. Kubernetes also uses its own verbs, which are often written in lowercase to distinguish
them from HTTP verbs.
Kubernetes uses the term
list
to describe the action of returning a
collection
of
resources, to distinguish it from retrieving a single resource which is usually called
a
get
. If you sent an HTTP GET request with the
?watch
query parameter,
Kubernetes calls this a
watch
and not a
get
(see
Efficient detection of changes
for more details).
For PUT requests, Kubernetes internally classifies these as either
create
or
update
based on the state of the existing object. An
update
is different from a
patch
; the
HTTP verb for a
patch
is PATCH.
